blockingIssues.description: Direct #node-* roadmap deep links only restore reducer state; they do not scroll or focus the atlas into view, so the intended node or invalid-link fallback is not visually apparent on first load as required by VAL-ROADMAP-006/007.

The scenario switcher only swaps read-only scenario tabs and assumptions; it never captures any user-entered tier, deposit, or other input state to preserve, so the VAL-TOKEN-007 requirement to preserve user inputs is not implemented.

The scarcity explainer says Lite holders are ineligible for strategy preview access, but the tier ladder data assigns Lite a non-zero preview priority, creating a contradictory whale-first rule that users cannot reconcile.

The cost-band models store dependency labels such as phase names instead of the promised stable phase IDs, so the architecture explorer is not actually stable-ID-backed and will break referential integrity if labels change.

The architecture flywheel marks Strategy Agents & Vaults as in progress while narrative, roadmap, and tokenomics still present strategy agents as planned, creating a cross-surface maturity mismatch that overstates readiness.

appliedUpdates.description: Extended .factory/library/testing.md with a factual Jest/jsdom note that page-level render trees importing client components that use IntersectionObserver or scrollIntoView need global mocks in jest.setup.ts.
Extended .factory/library/content-model.md with dependency conventions distinguishing readable narrative caveat dependencies from stable roadmap-node IDs and stable cost-band phase IDs.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the required browser-validation workflow to use the Factory agent-browser tool/skill path instead of trial-and-error raw CLI commands, and document the working interaction pattern (including non-default session usage and when eval-style fallbacks are needed for scrolling or precise interaction).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both atlas-interactive-roadmap-mindmap-experience and atlas-architecture-cost-bands-and-flywheel recorded repeated failed agent-browser command attempts before the workers discovered a usable invocation pattern during manual validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Record separate frontend-skill browser-guidance findings from the roadmap and architecture reviews.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate; consolidated into one systemic frontend-experience-worker skill recommendation

rejectedObservations.observation: Update .factory/services.yaml with browser automation command recipes.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because services.yaml is for operational commands and services, while browser interaction procedure belongs in skills or library guidance

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat the tokenomics worker's inaccurate followedProcedure=true handoff field as a new shared-state artifact update for this milestone.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because it is a worker-compliance issue rather than factual repo knowledge, and the broader process concern was already raised in foundation scrutiny guidance
